stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: TSMC, the leading semiconductor manufacturer, is facing significant challenges in meeting the soaring demand driven by AI.
Despite ongoing factory expansion, including in the US, TSMC has stated it has physical limits to how much demand it can support.
The shortage highlights the critical bottleneck in the global supply chain for advanced AI hardware.

stories.title: AI leaders call for tougher protections against AI-aided bioweapons
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Major AI industry leaders are uniting to address a critical biosecurity risk.
The leaders are urging US lawmakers to implement stricter regulations on AI use.
The primary concern is preventing the misuse of AI technology in developing biological weapons.

stories.title: Google ordered to put clearer links in AI search and let UK publishers opt out
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Google has been legally compelled to modify its AI Overviews to include clearer source links.
The ruling addresses concerns that Google's AI summaries were insufficiently transparent regarding their sources.
UK publishers have been granted the option to opt out of Google's AI indexing/display.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture, indicating focus on advanced conversational AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating focus on large language models (LLMs).
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance to modern deep learning infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud resources.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are openly available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open safety tooling.

stories.title: Amazon develops a warehouse robot that workers can speak to
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Amazon upgraded its warehouse robot, Proteus, to incorporate natural language interaction.
The robot now allows human workers to assign tasks using spoken commands instead of code.
This development signals Amazon's increasing pivot toward advanced AI-driven automation in logistics.

stories.title: Used Waymo robotaxi batteries become backup storage for power grids
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Used Waymo robotaxi batteries are being repurposed for energy storage.
This initiative will support power grid projects in California and Texas.
It represents a circular economy application for advanced EV/robotaxi battery technology.

stories.title: Google's new Gemma 4 12B model is designed to run on any laptop with 16GB of RAM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has released the Gemma 4 12B model.
The model is optimized to run locally on laptops equipped with 16GB of RAM.
Performance improvements are attributed to a new encoding scheme and token prediction method.

stories.title: Autonomous vehicles were supposed to cut traffic—what if they don't?
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Autonomous vehicle deployment (Waymo) may not solve traffic congestion as anticipated.
Data indicates Waymo's robotaxis are frequently operating with minimal or no passengers.
This challenges the core premise that autonomous fleets will inherently reduce urban traffic load.

stories.title: Audio Interaction Model
stories.author: Chunyan Miao
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Audio Interaction Model' to unify current siloed audio models (ASR, voice chat) into a single, online LALM.
The core concept is an 'always-on perceive-decide-respond loop' enabling real-time interaction with sound and environment.
The research introduces 'Audio-Interaction' as the realization of this unified, interactive audio paradigm.

stories.title: Who Needs Labels? Adapting Vision Foundation Models With the Metadata You Already Have
stories.author: Camille Couprie
bullet_points: Proposes a label-free method (FINO) to adapt large vision foundation models to specialized scientific domains.
Addresses the limitations of standard supervised fine-tuning in data-scarce, specialized settings.
Leverages existing metadata in a self-supervised manner to guide representation adaptation without requiring manual labels.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM deployment.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The update facilitates the use of proprietary/API-based models alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Major platforms (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok) are increasing content authentication efforts.
These platforms are implementing automatic labeling for AI-generated media (images, videos, music).
The initiative aims to help users distinguish between AI-generated and human-created content.

stories.title: Nintendo confirms it will sell a new Switch 2 with replaceable battery in the EU
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Nintendo is planning to release the Switch 2 in the EU with a feature allowing users to replace the battery.
This change is a direct response to upcoming EU regulations set to take effect on February 18th, 2027.
The move highlights the growing regulatory focus on product repairability within the European Union.

stories.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dashlane has experienced a vault theft incident, prompting user confusion.
The official security advisory from Dashlane is criticized for lacking crucial details.
The company has maintained a policy of silence regarding the specifics of the breach.

stories.title: Inside Meta's attempts to play catch-up with AI
stories.author: Hannah Murphy, Financial Times
bullet_points: The article discusses Meta's ongoing efforts to improve its Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
There is expressed skepticism regarding Meta's ability to match the advancements of its competitors.
The piece focuses on the competitive landscape within the AI industry.

stories.title: Streaming Communication in Multi-Agent Reasoning
stories.author: Ying-Cong Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the latency bottleneck in multi-agent reasoning systems caused by the 'generate-then-transfer' paradigm.
Introduces StreamMA, a novel system that streams reasoning steps to downstream agents immediately, enabling pipeline parallelism.
Demonstrates that this pipelining not only reduces latency but also surprisingly improves the overall effectiveness of multi-step reasoning.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of standard Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), which relies only on binary correctness.
Proposes using a distributional variant of DAgger (a classic imitation learning algorithm) to leverage rich feedback.
Rich feedback includes execution traces, tool outputs, expert corrections, and model self-evaluations, allowing for more nuanced learning.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute when language models fail reasoning tasks.
It posits that failed inference traces contain crucial information about the *type* of failure (e.g., sampling error vs. structural weakness).
The core contribution is proposing that these traces encode a 'recoverability structure' to guide future model improvements.

stories.title: GeM-NR: Geometry-Aware Multi-View Editing for Nonrigid Scene Changes
stories.author: Fredrik Kahl
bullet_points: The paper introduces GeM-NR, a method for geometry-aware multi-view editing specifically designed for nonrigid scene changes.
Current generative models often struggle with nonrigid edits because existing methods are limited to rigid structure preservation or specific tasks (like object removal).
This work advances the field toward more general 3D content generation and customization by addressing the limitations of current editing techniques.

stories.title: Continual Visual and Verbal Learning Through a Child's Egocentric Input
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces BabyCL, a continual multimodal learning framework designed to mimic how children learn.
Addresses a limitation in prior work where neural networks required cycling through shuffled data, unlike real-world continuous experience.
Utilizes the SAYCam dataset to process egocentric video input in a single chronological pass for visual and verbal learning.

stories.title: Arithmetic Pedagogy for Language Models
stories.author: Hokky Situngkir
bullet_points: The research explores applying human mathematics pedagogy (specifically the GASING method) to improve language model arithmetic reasoning.
The core methodology involves operationalizing arithmetic operations as computational procedures and serializing their execution traces into Chain-of-Thought (CoT) supervision.
The study demonstrates this approach using a small GPT-2 decoder (86M parameters), suggesting potential for targeted training improvements.

stories.title: Every web-data tool says it's "built for AI." I asked the AI.
stories.author: dovzhikova
bullet_points: The article investigates the claims of various web-data tools regarding their AI readiness.
The author tested five different AI engines using 198 real buyer questions for validation.
This suggests a practical, hands-on evaluation of AI integration in existing web-data solutions.

stories.title: Run AI Coding Agents Safely with Docker Sandboxes
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article addresses the security risks associated with running AI coding agents.
It proposes using Docker sandboxes as a method to safely execute these agents.
The core problem is preventing untrusted AI agents from executing dangerous commands or modifying systems.
